programmes.show_description: Notorious gang banger Baby D has placed a hit on Krystal from a rival gang - can the guards protect her? Plus, if pregnant heroin addict Vanessa gives birth in jail, she'll lose custody.
Cat and Mouse: With Samantha's family desperate for answers, Teddy sits down to answer Frederick's questions. As new information comes to light, one of Teddy's former students comes forward.

One Way Ticket: The first wave of new recruits make the mammoth journey from the UK to Australia - including young mum Anna Miller, her husband and three kids - and grapple with life down under. S1 Ep
Crikey, it's Christmas!: Ben works his first day at Perth station, which coincides with Christmas Day. Anna's family celebrate Christmas at the beach while she sees in the New Year on the beat.

For this family Mystery Box challenge, the cooks have 75 minutes to recreate a dish chosen by their family.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien hope a clay pigeon trap, a searchlight and a Peugeot 205 will help make a profit for their owners.
Duxford: Home renovation series. Nick Knowles and the team travel to Duxford, where they help a couple whose son has a rare form of liver cancer.
Bristol: The DIY SOS team and volunteers in Bristol help to transform the home of 24-year-old Ryan, who has spent the last five years in hospital 50 miles away from his family.

Kylo & Sasha: Kylo, a Rough Collie puppy from Glasgow, suffers from front leg deformities, while Golden Retriever Sasha also has a deformed foot where her toes haven't properly developed.
Rolo, Astrid & Jeff: The practice has an unusual patient when ferret Astrid breaks her leg while doing a 'war dance', and puddle-loving Labrador Jeff has a developmental elbow disease.
Dudley: Nick Knowles and the team join forces with local tradespeople to assist a family in Dudley, who want a house extension to help care for their children.
Duxford: Home renovation series. Nick Knowles and the team travel to Duxford, where they help a couple whose son has a rare form of liver cancer.
Merida, Elmo & Brodie: Rough Collie Merida is diagnosed at 12 weeks with a developmental deformity, while feisty six-year-old cat Elmo is referred after being hit by a car.
Hagar, Loki & Nutty: A French bulldog has an exploded disc and faces a high-risk operation, while a cat called Nutty has a shattered radius after being shot with an air gun.
Bear, Bluebell & Henry: English mastiff Bear visits Noel after suffering a painful ruptured cruciate ligament in his knee, while Siamese cat Bluebell is rushed in after being hit by a car.
Piers confronts convicted killer Mark Riebe, who has confessed multiple times to the abduction and murder of thirteen women in Florida.
At Missouri's Crossroads Correctional Facility, Piers comes face-to-face with Lorenzo Gilyard - the Kansas City Strangler.
Not the Right Fit: The final wave of UK recruits arrive. Ben is unsure whether he'll stay in Australia, and Jay has his first day out on the beat and is apprehensive about carrying a weapon.
The Holiday is Over: From first days on the beat to stressful house-hunting, the reality of life in Perth hits our rookie cops. Ben is torn between missing his family and his new amazing life.
Shopping from Home.
The cooks travel to Sydney to meet Peter Gilmore and watch him create squid, scallops and pipis on crispy eggplant with XO sauce - and then recreate the dish from memory.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel to East Sussex in their hunt to discover valuable items 'lost' in people's homes, with their finds including some Victorian street lamps.
The Million Pound Build for Children in Need: The team head to Swansea to build a centre and supported housing for young people in care and leaving care for the Roots Foundation Wales.
Arundel: Nick Knowles meets a West Sussex wife and mother-of-four who was paralysed in a bicycle accident in the summer of 2015, and needs her home adapting for her wheelchair.

Dexter, Driver & Bailey: Noel treats handsome Dobermann Dexter, who has a serious spinal condition, and feisty feline Driver, who's been hit by a car, resulting in hip and knee injuries.
Moon, Milo & Ollie: Eight-year-old Dalmatian Milo's squashed spinal cord causes him constant pain, and Noel attempts to fuse four-year-old Shih Tzu Ollie's elbow in order to save his leg.
Telford: In Shropshire, three schoolchildren, the DIY SOS team and hundreds of volunteers help 12-year-old Matthew return home from hospital for the first time in three years.
The Million Pound Build for Children in Need: The team head to Swansea to build a centre and supported housing for young people in care and leaving care for the Roots Foundation Wales.
A teenager is found with multiple stab wounds in the street near his home, an amateur tree surgeon is badly hurt when a branch falls on his leg and two schoolgirls are run over.
Heart-warming documentary series. Ryan and Keely are teenage sweethearts, while Katie and Steve's baby will need surgery a few hours after birth.
Maternity series set on a buzzing ward in Leeds. Becky and John are naming their baby after their favourite Star Wars character, while Leah's partner will miss the birth.
